snp2.14:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
snp2.14:1.3:
Then Dhammika the lay follower, together with 500 other lay followers,
approached the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
As he was sitting there, he addressed the Blessed One in verses:
snp2.14:2.2: How-acting does one become a good disciple—
snp2.14:2.3: either one gone from home into homelessness,
snp2.14:2.4: or a lay follower with a home?
snp2.14:3.1: For you discern the destination & future course
snp2.14:3.2: of the world along with its devas
snp2.14:3.3: —there is no one equal to you in seeing the subtle goal;
snp2.14:3.4: for they call you foremost, awakened.
snp2.14:4.1: Understanding all knowledge the whole Dhamma,
snp2.14:4.2: you proclaim it to beings with sympathetic mind.
snp2.14:4.3: Your roof opened up, All-around Eye,
snp2.14:4.4: you, stainless, illumine the whole world.
snp2.14:5.1: He came to your presence— Erāvaṇa, the nāga king,
snp2.14:5.3: Having consulted with you, having heard, he understood and, satisfied,
snp2.14:6.3: To him, too, you spoke when asked.
snp2.14:6.4: And he too, having heard, was satisfied.
snp2.14:7.1: And these sectarians, debaters by habit,
snp2.14:7.3: don’t overtake you in terms of discernment,
snp2.14:7.4: as a person standing doesn’t catch up with one going quickly.
snp2.14:8.1: And these brahmans, debaters by habit,
snp2.14:8.2: any elderly brahmans, and any others who consider themselves debaters,
snp2.14:8.3: all depend on you for the meaning.
snp2.14:9.1: For this Dhamma is subtle & blissful.
snp2.14:9.2: This, Blessed One, well set-forth by you:
snp2.14:9.3: We all want to hear it.
snp2.14:9.4: Tell it to us, Excellent Awakened, when asked.
snp2.14:10.1: All these monks are sitting together—
snp2.14:10.2: and the lay followers, right there—to hear.
snp2.14:10.3: Let them listen to the Dhamma awakened to by one who is stainless,
snp2.14:10.4:
as the devas listen to Vasavant’s well-spoken word.”
The Buddha:
snp2.14:11.1:
“Listen to me, monks.
I will let you hear
snp2.14:11.2:
the Dhamma of polishing away.
Live by it, all of you.
May those who are thoughtful, seeing the purpose,
snp2.14:11.3: partake of the duties proper for one gone forth.
snp2.14:12.1: A monk should surely not wander at the wrong time.
snp2.14:12.2: He should go for alms in the village at the right time.
snp2.14:12.3: For attachments get attached to one going at the wrong time.
snp2.14:12.4: That’s why they don’t wander at the wrong time, the awakened.
snp2.14:13.1: Sights, sounds, tastes, smells, & tactile sensations
snp2.14:13.4: one should, at the right time, enter for alms.
snp2.14:14.1: Having received alms in proper season,
snp2.14:14.2: a monk, returning alone, should sit down in solitude.
snp2.14:14.3: Pondering what’s inside, his mind-state collected,
snp2.14:14.4: he should not let his heart wander outside.
snp2.14:15.1: If he should converse with a disciple,
snp2.14:15.3: he should utter the exquisite Dhamma,
snp2.14:15.4: and not divisive speech or disparagement of others.
snp2.14:16.2: We don’t praise those of limited discernment.
snp2.14:16.3: Attachments get attached on account of this & that,
snp2.14:16.4:
for they send their minds far away from there.
Having heard the Dhamma taught by the One Well-Gone, the disciple of foremost discernment, having considered it,
snp2.14:17.1: should resort to almsfood, a dwelling, a place to sit & lie down,
snp2.14:17.2:
and water for washing dust from his robe.
So a monk should stay unsmeared by these things—
snp2.14:18.1: almsfood, a dwelling, a place to sit & lie down,
snp2.14:18.2: and water for washing dust from his robe—
snp2.14:18.4: like a water-drop on a lotus.
snp2.14:19.1: As for the householder protocol, I will tell you how-acting
snp2.14:19.2: one becomes a good disciple, since the entire monk-practice
snp2.14:19.3:
can’t be managed by those wealthy in property.
Laying aside violence toward all living creatures, both the firm & unfirm in the world,
snp2.14:20.1: one should not kill a living being, nor have it killed,
snp2.14:21.1: Then the disciple should avoid consciously (taking) what’s not given,
snp2.14:21.3: should not have it taken nor condone its taking.
snp2.14:21.4: He should avoid all (taking of) what’s not given.
snp2.14:22.1: The observant person should avoid uncelibate behavior
snp2.14:22.2: like a pit of glowing embers.
snp2.14:22.3: But if he’s incapable of celibate behavior,
snp2.14:22.4: he should not transgress with the wife of another.
snp2.14:23.1: When gone to an audience hall or assembly,
snp2.14:23.2: or one-on-one, he should not tell a lie,
snp2.14:23.3: nor have it told, nor condone its being told.
snp2.14:23.4:
He should avoid every untruth.
Any householder who approves of this Dhamma
snp2.14:24.3: nor have others drink it, nor condone its being drunk,
snp2.14:24.4: knowing that it ends in madness.
snp2.14:25.1: For from intoxication, fools do evil things
snp2.14:25.2: and get others, heedless, to do them.
snp2.14:25.3: One should avoid this opening to demerit—
snp2.14:26.1: One should not kill a living being,
snp2.14:26.2: take what’s not given, tell a lie, nor be a drinker.
snp2.14:26.3: One should abstain from uncelibate behavior— sexual intercourse—should not eat at night,
snp2.14:26.4: a meal at the wrong time,
snp2.14:27.1: should not wear a garland or use scents,
snp2.14:27.2: should sleep on a bed, on the ground, or on a mat,
snp2.14:27.3: For this, they say, is the eightfold uposatha,
snp2.14:27.4: proclaimed by the Awakened One who has gone to the end of suffering & stress.
snp2.14:28.1: Then, having kept, well-accomplished, the eightfold uposatha
snp2.14:28.2: on the fourteenth, fifteenth, & eighth of the fortnight,
snp2.14:28.3: and on special days of the fortnight, with clear & confident mind, the observant person
snp2.14:29.4: should share food & drink, as is proper, with the Saṅgha of monks.
snp2.14:30.1: One should righteously support mother & father,
snp2.14:30.3: One heedful in this householder protocol
snp2.14:30.4: goes to the devas called Self-radiant.”
